batch_summary: In Monica's apartment, Rachel asks to move in with Phoebe, only to discover Phoebe has a mysterious roommate named Denise whom no one has ever heard of. Joey offers Rachel a room at his place, but she declines after he mentions "naked Thursdays." Later, at the coffee shop, Phoebe confronts Ross, asking how Rachel reacted when he told her they were still married, catching Ross completely off guard.
cumulative_summary: The episode opens in the apartment where Chandler, Monica, Joey, and Phoebe are hanging out. When Rachel enters looking for a new place to live, she asks to move in with Phoebe. However, Phoebe reveals she already has a roommate named Denise. The group is shocked, having never heard of Denise, but Phoebe insists she talks about her all the time. Joey then offers Rachel a room at his apartment, but his mention of "naked Thursdays" quickly prompts her to decline and seek her own place. Later, at the coffee shop, the narrative shifts to Ross. Phoebe sits down with him and casually asks how Rachel reacted when he told her they were still married. Ross, who clearly has not broken the news to Rachel yet, is left speechless, setting up a major conflict regarding their ongoing, secret marriage.

batch_summary: At the coffee shop, Phoebe confronts Ross about not telling Rachel they are still married. Ross defends his silence, claiming he only comforted Rachel as a friend. However, Phoebe teases him about smelling Rachel's "coconutty" hair, accusing him of still being in love with her, which Ross vehemently denies. Meanwhile, back at the apartment, Monica excitedly pitches her elaborate plans to turn Rachel's soon-to-be-vacant bedroom into a high-end guest room once Chandler moves in.

batch_summary: Monica and Chandler have their first fight about moving in together when Chandler suggests turning Rachel's room into a game room. batch_summary: At Central Perk, Rachel laments her disastrous apartment hunt, even reading Joey's anonymous "non-ugly" roommate ad.
